176137.name: Annual consumption-based CO2 emissions per GDP (kg per $PPP)
176137.description: Annual consumption-based emissions of carbon dioxide (CO2), measured in kilograms per dollar of GDP (2011 international-$). Consumption-based emissions are national or regional emissions which have been adjusted for trade (i.e. territorial/production emissions minus emissions embedded in exports, plus emissions embedded in imports). If a country's consumption-based emissions are higher than its production emissions it is a net importer of carbon dioxide. Consumption-based CO2 emissions have been converted by Our World in Data from tonnes of carbon to tonnes of CO2 using a conversion factor of 3.664.

display.name: Carbon intensity: annual consumption-based CO₂ emissions per unit GDP

176137.datasetName: Global Carbon Budget - Global Carbon Project (2020)
176137.s_name: Our World in Data based on the Global Carbon Project and Maddison Project Database 2020 (Bolt and van Zanden (2020))

source.name: Our World in Data based on the Global Carbon Project and Maddison Project Database 2020 (Bolt and van Zanden (2020))
source.dataPublishedBy: Our World in Data based on the Global Carbon Project and Maddison Project Database 2020 (Bolt and van Zanden (2020))
source.dataPublisherSource: Friedlingstein et al. (2020) and Maddison Project Database 2020 (Bolt and van Zanden (2020))
source.additionalInfo:
The Global Carbon Budget dataset is available at https://folk.universitetetioslo.no/roberan/GCB2020.shtml (1750-2019) and https://doi.org/10.18160/GCP-2020 (1959-2019).

Full reference for the Global Carbon Budget - Global Carbon Project (2020) dataset: Friedlingstein et al, Global Carbon Budget 2020, Earth Syst. Sci. Data, 12, 3269–3340, https://doi.org/10.5194/essd-12-3269-2020, 2020.

Our World in Data have renamed the category "bunker fuels" as "International transport" for improved clarity, which includes emissions from international aviation and shipping.

GDP figures are sourced from Bolt, Jutta and Jan Luiten van Zanden (2020), “Maddison style estimates of the evolution of the world economy. A new 2020 update”. Link: https://www.rug.nl/ggdc/historicaldevelopment/maddison/releases/maddison-project-database-2020

Consumption-based CO2 emissions have been constructed by the Global Carbon Project using the methodology described in:

Peters, GP, Minx, JC, Weber, CL and Edenhofer, O 2011. Growth in emission transfers via international trade from 1990 to 2008. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences 108, 8903-8908. http://www.pnas.org/content/108/21/8903.abstract
